baseline.notes: BQR work is implementation history only. No BQR phase is `accepted` under BQC rules. Open findings remain from the 2026-07-16 validation report until closed by BQC evidence.

entries.title: Containment and rebaseline (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Not BQC-accepted. Containment defects remain.

entries.title: Architecture and schema (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Presence/architecture tests incomplete relative to BQC.

entries.title: Durable runtime (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Tracer-bullet durable path only; not full family coverage.

entries.title: Source lifecycle and region (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Lifecycle/region incomplete at production depth.

entries.title: Auth privacy containment (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Policy not authoritative everywhere.

entries.title: Experience verification (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Browser gates shallow; residual soft.

entries.title: Ops and scale (historical BQR)
entries.notes: Historical. Templates/checklists without executed staging scale proof.

entries.title: Pilot (historical BQR)
entries.blockedDependency: human pilot + real properties; BQC-0 through BQC-8 acceptance first
entries.notes: Superseded as completion path by BQC-9. Pilot remains human-gated.

entries.title: Truthful rebaseline and containment
entries.notes: All five slices (0.1–0.5) implementation_complete. Awaiting independent review of evidence plus the staging stop-control rehearsal steps (docs/operations/bqc0-stop-control-rehearsal.md) before acceptance. Findings close only on accepted evidence per slice.

entries.title: Machine-readable program status
entries.notes: Status schema + CI validator shipped. Not BQC-accepted until independent review of evidence.

entries.title: Correct portal capability taxonomy
entries.notes: STD-P0-01 fixed in mapping + portal server asserts. Evidence: portal-capability-taxonomy.test.ts. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Restrict test-only capability overrides
entries.notes: SPEC-P0-03 containment portion addressed: BETA_E2E_GLOBAL_CAPABILITIES refuses to take effect outside NODE_ENV=test or explicit BETA_E2E_EXECUTION_IDENTITY — true startup refusal in the worker entry, and fail-closed at first capability evaluation in every process via the lazy getStore() net (proven: built production server 500s with a leaked override; dev server 500s /register; both boot normally with identity or without override). Blocked-capability boot assertion + policy version + tenant-free manifest logged at worker startup. Evidence: capability-boot-guard.test.ts (18 tests). Nitro server/plugins are inert in this build (B0.7 security-headers equally affected — flagged for BQC-6/7), so web has no process-startup hook. Authoritative persisted policy remains BQC-2 scope. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Confirm operational stop controls
entries.notes: Six stop controls documented and exercised (docs/operations/bqc0-stop-control-rehearsal.md). New: per-capability kill list (BETA_CAPABILITIES_OFF comma form — runbook §11 previously prescribed a value the code silently ignored), capability re-checks in sync/import/publish job handlers, ops:queue pause/resume quarantine (jobs preserved). Boot manifest records kill posture. Temporary boot-time containment; removal slice is BQC-2 persisted policy. Open honestly: staging rehearsal steps (live Redis pause, kill-list under load), per-property stop and DLQ deferred to BQC-2/BQC-3. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Re-run and pin the baseline
entries.notes: Baseline pinned at 927614bce (migration 0011, lockfile 948a751d) via scripts/bqc/run-baseline.ts — 13/17 gates pass, results verbatim in evidence/baseline-2026-07-17T08-33-43/. Failures recorded with ownership, not excused: dependency-audit 5 known vulns (BQC-7), fallow dead-code/health baseline state (BQC-5), e2e-full 6 local registration-path failures root-caused to node:crypto client leak = STD-P2-01 (BQC-5/BQC-6; CI green on same SHA, timing dimension to isolate). Reproducible via pnpm bqc:run-baseline. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Google source-data governance
entries.notes: All seven slices (1.1–1.7) implementation_complete. Awaiting independent review of evidence before acceptance.

entries.title: Disconnect/property/org purge
entries.notes: Same lifecycle module exercised for all three purges (purge-source-content.ts, bounded via the retention executor with evidence). Disconnect: bounded review purge per connection (replies cascade per batch) + redactForDisconnect removes tokens/email/account id/scopes — connection row stays as content-free audit fact. Property purge: inbox rows + reviews deleted in bounded batches BEFORE propertyRepo.hardDelete (no unbounded cascade; gbp_cache via FK). Org purge: bounded review erasure across the org. Integration-proven on real PostgreSQL (bounded batching, exact scoping, reply cascade, evidence rows). CONTEXT.md FK-cascade claim corrected. Audit evidence preserved: events, activity log, retention_runs rows are never deleted by purges. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Safe erasure and valid retention SQL
entries.notes: Invalid DELETE...LIMIT replaced by bounded id-IN-subquery executor (integration-proven incl. composite keys); broken unused outbox purge methods removed. retention-sweep job scheduled daily (background queue, offset after purge) with a 9-subject rule registry: outbox published 30d, receipts 30d, sync/refresh runs 30d, webhook receipts 30d, notifications 90d, email queue terminal 90d, activity 90d, gbp_cache expiry. Content-free evidence per subject in retention_runs (migration 0013): subject/timestamps/counts/outcome/errorCode/policyVersion. Purge records reviews.purge evidence; emit-before-delete stop-before-canonical already pinned (purge test). Reads fail closed at hard expiry independently of sweep completion (1.2/1.4 eligibility). Log hygiene fixed at source: GBP error context stores status+bodyBytes (was raw body), webhook logs messageId/booleans (was raw body/decoded names), check-db prints identifiers+clocks (was reviewer content). Registry updated. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Bounded refresh with progress and backpressure
entries.notes: One-shot 5000-row scan replaced with keyset-cursor batches ((contentExpiresAt,id) ordered, integration-proven: ordered walk, no skips/repeats, no re-scan behind cursor). Sweep persists run cursor/counts/oldest-due/failures/terminal state to review_refresh_runs (migration 0012, applied to scratch DB). Failed enqueue → run 'failed' + throw, cursor held — never acknowledged as success (queue retry re-processes; sync upserts idempotent). Budget: 500-row batches × 10 per run; hourly schedule with resume (keeps pace with ~16.7K/day refresh-due at target scale). Oldest-due alert: warn log under 2d lead + oldestDueAgeSeconds on /api/health/metrics. Purge safety: only successful fetches advance the clock (1.3). Note: drizzle meta chain is broken (snapshots 0004–0010 are duplicate copies of 0003, 0011 snapshot missing) — generate-flow unusable; recorded under STD-P2-02 (BQC-5); migration hand-written + journal entry, db:migrate verified. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Centralize eligible reads
entries.notes: review.publicApi is now the governed read interface (createEligibleReads): typed unavailable outcomes for serving reads; satisfies inbox ReviewLookupPort and metric ReviewRatingLookupPort directly — per-context eligibility adapters deleted (one rule, one owner: isContentEligibleForRead). Last two serving leaks closed: dashboard getRecentReviews excludes expired/clock-less content in SQL; staff recent-activity uses findRecentEligibleByPropertyId (SQL predicate). Aggregates (avg rating, distributions, counts) remain by classification (irreversible, cannot reconstruct raw content) — recorded for reviewer. Operations paths (sync/refresh/purge/reply-publish) read rows directly by design. Integration-proven predicates (review repo + dashboard adapter + boundary case). Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Correct successful-refetch persistence
entries.notes: PostgreSQL upsert now advances lastFetchedAt/contentExpiresAt/contentHash/source timestamps on every successful fetch; firstFetchedAt preserved by omission. Previously the ON CONFLICT SET dropped all lifecycle fields — the fetch clock never advanced on refetch (content would expire despite successful refreshes). False-green closed from both sides: unit asserts clocks passed to upsert (sync-reviews.test.ts:360), integration proves the real adapter persists them (review.repository.test.ts BQC-1.3 describe). Hash-stable refetch emits no semantic event (BQR-3.4, existing); content-free refresh fact added (SyncReviewsResult.refreshed, logged by the sync job; hash-stable test asserts refreshed:1). Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Remove raw inbox/activity/transport copies
entries.notes: Raw inbox copies eliminated: writes stopped, reads resolve live via the eligibility-enforcing review lookup (isContentEligibleForRead; expired/clock-less fail closed with typed unavailable outcomes); bounded idempotent null-backfill integration-proven. review.created/updated payloads identifier-only (rating removed; metric reads rating at consume time, records nothing when expired). review.updated inbox consumer removed. Activity facts content-free (googleEmail/note-text vectors gone). Notification templates content-free (no raw content to Resend). Registry updated; doc §5 records remaining vectors per owning slice. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Complete field-and-copy inventory
entries.notes: Verified inventory + executable classification published (bqc1-field-copy-inventory.md). Machine registry (protected-field-registry.ts, 46 entries) guarded by a test that fails when a protected field is introduced without classification (schema columns incl. outbox barrel-excluded tables, outbox event payload fields, job payload scan). Backup/PITR decision recorded as open prerequisite (security/privacy owner) before real data. Worst unmanaged copies ranked for 1.2/1.6/1.7. Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Authoritative authorization and capabilities
entries.notes: All seven slices (2.1–2.7) implementation_complete: catalogue, persisted policy state, grant wiring, ExecutionPolicy interactive cutover, delayed/system contract, dark containment, policy operations. Awaiting independent review of evidence before acceptance. SPEC-P0-03 remains evidence_pending until BQC-3 delayed integration and BQC-6 browser evidence pass (ownership model); STD-P1-02 interactive portions await the same review.

entries.title: Policy operations
entries.notes: Authenticated, least-privilege policy administration (phase §2.7): 7 server fns (identity/server/policy-admin.ts) — getPolicyStateFn (content-free org state read), setOrgCapabilityFn (allowlist/clear non-core capabilities; core/blocked/unknown rejected), setOrgSuspensionFn + setPropertySuspensionFn (reason + ticket required), grantPropertyAccessFn (reason + ticket + optional expiry + membership required), revokePropertyAccessFn (reason required), explainPolicyDecisionFn (read-only diagnostic: per-layer breakdown without PII or secret config — ids/booleans/reason codes only). New owner-only policy.admin permission (PM holds organization.update, so a dedicated permission was required for least privilege — proven: PM/Staff deny permission_denied, AccountAdmin passes). Every mutation writes a content-free audit row (actorType/executionKind 'operator', reason+ticket in the row). Validation errors map to 400. isPermissionAction hardened: permission discrimination is now the exhaustive capability map, not a prefix regex (caught a real skip). listAllCapabilities() added (ALL_CAPABILITIES was missing the 8 surface caps). Global kill switches stay env-managed (BQC-0.4); general runtime/redrive/repair operator commands are BQC-7. 7 integration + 3 least-privilege tests green. Not claimed: admin UI (surface is server fns; UI wiring later), cross-org operator admin (BQC-7); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Dark-context policy and interactive containment
entries.notes: All 40 dark server fns (portal 24, team 4, goal 6, leaderboard 2, badge 4) migrated from assertBetaCapability/requireAuthorized to requireExecutionAllowed — dark denial now flows through the ExecutionPolicy (capability-first: blocked/non-core deny before permission). Route-level containment: gateDarkRoute on 9 route files (team, leaderboard, progress, recognition, goals layout, teams layout, portals ×3) redirects to a new intentional /unavailable experience (never a partially live shell); routeTree regenerated; catalogue row added. Negative matrix (8 dark caps × 4 layers: policy deny with stable reasons, route redirect, guest/global gate, delayed-contract deny for dark job actions + AI) — policy/server/command negative tests, no positive E2E opened by overrides (phase §2.6). Contract fixtures now 11 incl. goal/badge reconcile dark-denial. Superseded path REMOVED: authorization-policy.ts (requireAuthorized/authorize/checkAuthorization) + its tests deleted after zero production callers remained; permission→capability map lives in capability-for-permission.ts; BQC-0.2 taxonomy proof re-seamed through the ExecutionPolicy (stronger); 2.4 shadow record kept with recorded old decisions; ADR 0033 addendum records the supersession; boundary + dark-enforcement + ADR-presence architecture tests updated. Not claimed: delayed-runtime dark denial (BQC-3), direct-navigation/browser evidence (BQC-6), ungated in-process dark consumers (badge/goal/leaderboard handlers on metric.recorded — BQC-3 owns runtime denial), per-org allowlisted route promotion (2.7); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Delayed/system policy contract
entries.notes: The contract workers/consumers/schedules will authorize delayed execution against (IMPLEMENTS; BQC-3 owns call-site integration — no worker/consumer/schedule edits here, per ownership model). system-execution-policy.ts: normalized request (named system principal, catalogue action, org/property, execution kind, initiator, purpose, policyVersionAtEnqueue, correlation) → typed outcome allow / deny(reason) / stale_context. Rules proven by 12 fixture tests + real-PG wiring: decisions always computed from CURRENT policy (stale allow never overrides); external-effect actions (catalogue externalEffect: GBP sync/publish/import, S3, email) perform a version-gated strong read immediately before deciding — suspension written NOW denies NOW, proven on real PG without waiting the 5s bound; catalogue-derived scope validation (property action without propertyId → missing_scope; no org → missing_scope); stale_context annotates version skew, never overrides; role/grant checks stay at the interactive boundary (capability+suspension+consent re-checked); unknown actions deny closed. Contract data derived from the 2.1 catalogue (capability merge: strictest gate wins — multi-row actions). Content-free audit per decision (JobRuntime consumes the result; rows land in policy_decision_audit). Deterministic fixtures exported (system-execution-policy.fixtures.ts) for BQC-3 integration tests. Every delayed entry point recorded as awaiting BQC-3: catalogue policyIntegration='pending_bqc3' on all 40 job/consumer/schedule rows, guard-enforced. Composition installs the contract (initDelayedExecutionPolicy in policy-store-init, sharing the 2.2 refresh). Not claimed: worker/consumer/schedule call-site integration (BQC-3.2), job envelope schema (BQC-3), operator-identity for delayed work (BQC-3.2/BQC-7); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Interactive production cutover
entries.notes: ExecutionPolicy (src/shared/auth/execution-policy.ts, EXECUTION_POLICY_VERSION bqc-2.4) is the one fail-closed interactive decision point: normalized request (principal/action/org/property/kind/purpose/time/correlation) → allow or typed deny; role permissions, grants, allowlist, suspension, capability, consent, caches, and content-free decision audit hidden inside (audit rows land in policy_decision_audit, fire-and-forget, decisions never blocked). System/operator principals deny unsupported_principal pending the 2.5 contract. Cutover: 67 requireAuthorized call sites across 28 files in the 9 enabled contexts migrated to requireExecutionAllowed (codemod); 10 canonicalOnly fns wired (incl. F045 — createOrganizationFn now asserts organization.create globally); 12 boundary calls now pass the actual target propertyId from input; indirectly-scoped targets stay enforced by the grant-backed use-case checks (2.3). Catalogue guard extended to recognize the new seam; authorize-server-boundary architecture test updated; 10 catalogue rows moved off canonicalOnly. Composition installs the policy with identity-owned grant/consent/audit deps (proven end-to-end on real PG: org-wide allow, scope_denied without grant, allow after grant, audit rows). Shadow record (synthetic identities): old vs new decisions identical except exactly the two old fail-open cases (assigned-scope, target property, no grant) — both now scope_denied; capability/permission denies identical. Regression fixed en route: persisted store allowlist for core capabilities now matches env parity (caught by the wiring test). Deferred: requireAuthorized deletion — still used by dark contexts (2.6); session fns (getSession/ensureActiveOrg etc.) stay session-scoped; public registration fns keep assertGlobalCapability; routes' beforeLoad can() UI gates unchanged. Not claimed: delayed/system integration (BQC-3.2), dark-context matrix (2.6), operator workflows (2.7); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Wire PropertyAccessGrant
entries.notes: Property scope now resolves ONLY from property_access_grant (ADR 0039 implemented): staffPublicApi.getAccessiblePropertyIds — the single lookup behind all ~30 property-access checks across property/portal/inbox/review/dashboard/goal/team/staff/activity — now delegates to the identity-owned grant adapter instead of staff_assignments. Money tests: staff_assignment WITHOUT grant does NOT authorize; grant WITHOUT staff_assignment DOES; revoked/expired never do. Missing scope = empty set = deny, never org-wide allow (fail-closed port: errors propagate). Cache keyed on policy_version (grants bump it in the same statement) — grant/revoke visible on the very next call; middleware propertySetsCache + helpers deleted (dead). Reconciliation: ops:reconcile-grants (report-first, --apply converts only clean rows, idempotent, source 'migration'; anomalies — org_mismatch, property_inactive, user_missing — reported, never converted). Discovery: checkAuthorization Layer 3 (assignedPropertyIds) had zero production callers — real enforcement was the staff lookup, now grant-backed. Last-owner protection verified member/role-only (no property/staff input), unchanged. Not claimed: ExecutionPolicy cutover of entry points (2.4), decision audit writes (2.4+), dark-context portal-participation visibility rewiring (2.6), grant kind hierarchy (post-beta PB1.1); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Persisted policy state
entries.notes: Authoritative persistence live (migration 0014, hand-written + journal — broken meta chain STD-P2-02): organization_policy (cohort+suspension), organization_capability/property_capability allowlists, property_policy, property_access_grant (scope/source/lifecycle; schema's first composite FK (org, property) → properties(org, id) — cross-tenant grants unrepresentable; one active grant per org/property/user), policy_consent (generic governed state only; no AI flows per phase §9), policy_decision_audit (content-free, no tenant FKs — evidence survives deletion), policy_version global counter. Every mutation bumps version in the same statement (CTE) — the invalidation contract. Composite store installed in both processes: env global posture (kill switch/e2e unchanged) + version-gated snapshot for tenant state; env allowlist/suspension unions in permanently (empty DB ⇒ identical behavior). Revocation/suspension bound = POLICY_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS (5s), integration-proven end-to-end (suspension invisible pre-refresh, denies post-refresh; allowlist row enables non-core cap post-refresh). Worker awaits first refresh; container.refreshPolicyStore() is the strong-read handle for side effects (2.5). ADR 0047 supersedes ADR 0032's deferred-table note. 18 integration + 8 unit tests green incl. constraint rejects (cross-tenant, duplicate active, enum CHECKs) and expiry semantics. Not claimed: grant reconciliation into decisions (2.3), ExecutionPolicy/decision-audit writes (2.3+), operator workflows (2.7); slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Canonical action/resource catalogue
entries.notes: 261 guard-verified rows covering every executable entry point: 129 server functions (authz pinned to extracted requireAuthorized/assert*Capability calls), 43 UI + 9 API routes, 19 jobs (capability gates pinned to registration/handler), 9 consumer modules (exact event-tag sets), 12 schedules (worker jobIds resolved), 40 operator commands (DIRECT-DB bypasses flagged). CI fails when any new entry point lacks a row or drifts from code; policy test executes every row's capability decision (blocked hard-deny, non-core deny-by-default, core allow). Canonical SystemAction vocabulary created for session/guest/machine/system/operator work. Surfaced for later slices (recorded, not fixed): activity surface gated via inbox.read → inbox.use instead of activity.use (2.4 remap); 17 canonicalOnly entry points with no mechanically checkable authz incl. createOrganizationFn F045 (2.4 wiring list); badge/goal/leaderboard in-process consumers ungated on core metric.recorded events (2.6); /api/public/click + google callback + gbp webhook ingress lack capability asserts (2.4/2.5). Narrative: bqc2-action-resource-catalogue.md. Not claimed: per-principal decision matrices, route beforeLoad drift detection (2.4/2.6). Pending independent review for accepted.

entries.title: Durable commands, consumers, and jobs
entries.notes: BQC-3.1 (event/job inventory + guard), BQC-3.2 (delayed policy integration), BQC-3.3 (review/reply atomic commands), BQC-3.4 (inbox atomic projections/commands), BQC-3.5 (remaining enabled families), BQC-3.6 (dispatcher/unknown-work correction), BQC-3.7 (relay/lease/ordering/retention) implementation_complete.

entries.title: Relay, lease, ordering, and retention hardening
entries.notes: Lease lifecycle proven against real PostgreSQL (6 integration tests): claim (SKIP LOCKED — two concurrent relays claim disjoint batches), RENEW (new ownership-guarded renewLease — the relay renews the unprocessed remainder every 10 publishes, killing the mid-batch lease-loss race), reclaim (expired lease claimable by any relay), release (markPublished clears lease). TWO PRE-EXISTING LATENT BUGS FIXED in the claim path: the claim CTE's SET used relation-qualified column names (PG rejects → claim never worked on real PG — latent because the dispatcher is off) and raw driver rows (snake_case) were cast to camelCase so every mapped field was undefined; the rewrite qualifies RETURNING columns and maps honestly. Relay: host-scoped identity relay-<hostname>-<pid>; dispatch jobs now carry attempts:3 + exponential backoff + jitter (previously neither retried nor quarantined — 3.6's quarantine never engaged); relay-side payload pre-validation REMOVED — the dispatcher is the single validation authority (its 3.6 UnrecoverableError quarantines poison) — the re-claim busy loop is dead. Envelope preservation (content-free, no migration): occurredAt (payload), recordedAt (row created_at via epoch extraction), correlationId (adapter re-attaches post-validation — it was STRIPPED at insert before), causationId/sourceAggregateVersion (null today — no producer sets them; honest note), region const 'unscoped' (BQC-4 owns re-scoping); old 8-field envelopes still parse. Ordering DEFINED in the catalogue: per-aggregate chronological enqueue (created_at) + state-idempotent consumers + receipt fencing; NO global ordering, NO execution-order guarantee (dispatcher concurrency 20); aggregate-version fencing unimplemented (no family versions aggregates). Retention: outbox rule re-keyed created_at → published_at (an event unpublished 29d then published was deleted ~1d later); per-run drain bounded at 100 batches/rule with capped signal + resume next run; migration-comment drift (7d/90d vs code 30d/30d) documented, migrations untouched. Alert substrate (warn-only structured logs; BQC-7 owns dispatch): health-metrics gained claimedCount, oldestClaimedAgeMs, stalledLeaseCount (unexpired lease older than 2× duration), expiredLeaseCount now backed by findExpiredLeases (its first caller — bounded ≤1000 scan), quarantine count/oldest-age; domain-events + quarantine queues added to depth reads (route + worker); health-check job evaluates named thresholds (oldest unpublished >15min, stalled >0, quarantine >0, oldest quarantined >24h). Deviations: findExpiredLeases backs expiredLeaseCount (stalled uses sibling SQL — expired vs unexpired semantics); expiredLeaseCount is a bounded estimate, not an exact count. Not claimed: abort-based timeout cancellation (zombie-vs-retry — handlers idempotent), leasedAt-not-cleared pre-existing column behavior, index-predicate drift (BQC-5), real alert dispatch (BQC-7). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Correct dispatcher and unknown-work behavior
entries.notes: Unknown-work and dispatcher behavior corrected per the §4 failure taxonomy. Unknown job at dispatch: warn+return (silent BullMQ COMPLETED) → error log + UnknownJobError thrown — burns attempts into the failure quarantine. Boot readiness (NEW src/shared/jobs/readiness.ts): worker boot FAILS when an enabled catalogue job lacks a handler, when a registered handler is uncatalogued, or — only when OUTBOX_DISPATCHER_ENABLED — when a catalogued durable consumer is unregistered (flag off = consumers intentionally inert, info log). Dispatcher: malformed envelope / schema failure → BullMQ UnrecoverableError (no retry; content-free reason — type:version fingerprint only); zero consumers for a catalogue-durable type → throw (deployment/config failure, redeploy fixes), zero for bus-only → debug+complete (lying 'will be retried' message gone); consumer exceptions now AGGREGATE and rethrow after the loop (all consumers still invoked; receipts protect succeeded consumers on redelivery — the failed consumer is actually retried now); terminal policy deny writes an 'obsolete' receipt (processed without effect — no migration, existing CHECK constraint) instead of re-evaluating forever. Failure quarantine (NEW failure-quarantine.ts): max-attempt jobs move to a dedicated 'quarantine' BullMQ queue (never processed = dead letter) with a content-safe envelope — data passes through ONLY for catalogue-known work (identifier-only by construction), else {redacted:true}; failedReason = error name + first message line ≤200 chars, no stack; policyReason carries gate deny reasons. Redrive (NEW scripts/ops/quarantine-redrive.ts, pnpm ops:quarantine list|redrive --apply; catalogue OPERATOR_ROWS row): moves the job back to its original queue with a fresh catalogue-policy attempt budget + redriveMetadata; redacted envelopes refused. Explicit per-job retry policy: JOB_FAMILY_ROWS gained timeoutMs; NEW job-policy.ts jobEnqueueOptions(jobName) = {attempts, backoff{type,delay,jitter:0.5} (BullMQ v5 BackoffOptions.jitter verified), } applied at ALL enqueue sites (review/integration/notification/portal/activity adapters + every worker schedule registration); catalogue-declared timeout enforced by a race in the gated dispatch (JobTimeoutError) — BullMQ v5 removed the job-level timeout opt (documented deviation; handlers NOT cancelled — zombie-vs-retry race, handlers stay idempotent; Abort threading is 3.7 follow-up); the worker-level custom backoffStrategy was REMOVED (it overrode job-level jitter). Not claimed: relay-enqueue retry opts + removeOnFail cap + unrecoverable-failure alerting (3.7), abort-based cancellation (3.7), dormant JobRuntime migration (stays dormant). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Migrate remaining enabled families
entries.notes: All 5 remaining enabled families migrated to atomic command stores (characterize → atomic command; durable-consumer registration + authoritative switch per family are BQC-3.9's cutover charter — recorded division). NEW stores: IdentityCommandStore (better-auth tables via app-owned drizzle writes — pre-existing precedent; invite/accept/cancel/remove/role-change/registerOrganization; org advisory lock + last-owner re-check inside the tx; invitation email now sent post-commit), StaffCommandStore (assign/unassign/updatePortals — whole portal diff + all facts in ONE tx), PropertyCommandStore (create/update/delete; all 3 property.created producers routed through it; the duplicate plain-emit integration adapter DELETED — also fixed a pre-existing double-emit), IntegrationCommandStore (connect/reconnect/disconnect/visibility/import-completed; disconnect: status+redaction+fact atomic, gbp_cache purge + retention executor stay outside with the cross-system comment), MetricCommandStore (recordMetric — the committed reading id now matches the fact). emitAndRecord now remains ONLY in dark contexts (21 sites, by design) — new atomic-family-outbox guard forbids it in the 5 migrated contexts (+review/inbox guards from 3.3/3.4). Schema corrections, each verified never-successfully-recorded (zero historical rows) and fixed in place at v1: metric.recorded recordedAt→occurredAt; staff.assigned/unassigned staffId→assignmentId/userId/portalId; member.role_changed gained memberUserId (the TARGET — activity reads it; the schema kept only the actor); NEW registrations: identity.organization.created, property.updated/deleted, integration connected/disconnected/visibility_changed (googleEmail deliberately excluded). Notification/activity verified consume-only (nothing to migrate); rollup refresh jobs characterized (idempotent recompute, no facts). Remaining orphans re-pointed to BQC-3.9 (consume-or-retire is a cutover decision). Real-PG proofs per context (forced outbox failure → no state row; happy path → both rows same eventId). Noted gaps with owners: invitation insert bypasses the drizzle mirror via raw SQL (mirror carries a speculative teamId column no real DB has — BQC-5 schema chain); every pre-existing err.code==='23505' check in the repo is broken by drizzle error wrapping (fixed only at the 2 touched sites — cross-cutting hardening finding); purge↔delete non-atomicity in deleteProperty + disconnect review-side purge command (retention machinery); notification row + email-queue row non-atomic (no fact — later); dark-context emitAndRecord (21 sites) + orphan consume-or-retire = BQC-3.9. Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Deepen inbox projections and commands
entries.notes: Inbox projections and commands fully atomic. NEW InboxCommandStore (infrastructure/inbox-command-store.ts): one PostgreSQL transaction per command = state mutation + outbox fact (+ receipt for projections); post-commit bus emit failure-isolated. All 7 inbox use cases migrated (create/update-status/bulk-status/assign/escalate/resolve/note) — emitAndRecord REMOVED from the inbox context (new atomic-inbox-outbox architecture guard forbids it); bulk fan-out now commits the bulk update + N per-item facts atomically (no partial fan-out); the use-case outboxRepo wiring gap (all 8 inbox events were bus-only in production) closed by construction. The 6 stale event schemas FIXED in place at v1 (staffId→assignedTo/previousAssignee, escalatedBy/resolvedBy→userId, authorId→userId+noteId, aggregate bulk→per-item bulkId shape) — justified: they never successfully recorded (every insert would have thrown) and zero historical rows exist; the producer/domain side was right. InboxNoteAdded.text DROPPED (events carry noteId, not text — no consumer read it). Projection applyOnce co-commits state + receipt + emitted facts: applyReviewCreatedOnce (ON CONFLICT idempotent; win → fact + applied, conflict → duplicate, missing review → obsolete), applyReviewExpiredOnce (guarded close; fact only when the transition lands — kills the crash window that could permanently lose status_changed), NEW applyReviewUpdatedOnce (metadata-only sourceDate/platform refresh — resolves the 3.1 review.updated orphan), NEW applyReplyPublishedOnce (milestone stamp + guarded close + fact). Durable consumers 2→4 (registration guard updated); bus handlers stay as the expand-phase dual path (dispatcher off in production). NEW rebuildInboxProjection use case: bounded keyset batches, idempotent, report-first, dryRun; Pass A reconciles existing review-sourced items (close-on-expiry via contentExpiresAt, missing reply milestones, auto-close only when the publish milestone was not yet stamped — never fights a user reopen), Pass B creates missing items WITHOUT created facts (repair, not new information); never touches assignment/escalation/notes, never deletes; feedback items out of scope (guest dark). Event rows carry repairCommand: rebuildInboxProjection. Real-PG proofs (7 integration tests): forced outbox/receipt failures leave no item row (rollback), duplicate delivery = 1 item/1 fact/1 receipt, rebuild heals + dryRun writes nothing + second run idempotent. Deviations: sequential test fake lives in src/shared/testing (fallow boundary: application tests can't import infrastructure); rebuild close criterion uses contentExpiresAt (converges with the purge end state); recordReceipt via single-statement tx (one code path). Not claimed: feedback rebuild (guest/portal promotion), inbox legacy content column removal (BQC-1.6/1.7 contraction), remaining CONTEXT.md staleness (docs debt), rebuild operator surface (BQC-7). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Deepen the review/reply command family
entries.notes: Review/reply command family fully atomic via new ReplyCommandStore (src/contexts/review/infrastructure/reply-command-store.ts): every fact-emitting reply transition (submit/approve/reject/markPublished/markPublishFailed) + mirror-synced reply upsert/delete + expired-review purge now commit state mutation + outbox row in ONE PostgreSQL transaction (guarded TOCTOU update inside the tx via shared buildReplySetClause; zero-row race → null, no outbox row, no emit), post-commit bus emit failure-isolated. Crash contract proven on real PG (4 integration tests): forced outbox-insert failure → state row absent (no split); happy path → both rows same eventId; purge → delete + expired fact atomic. emitAndRecord REMOVED from all 7 review-family call sites (reply-operations ×5, sync-reviews mirror, purge job); the production wiring gap (replyDeps never received outboxRepo — review.reply.* + review.expired were bus-only despite the 3.1 catalogue) is closed by the store itself. Publication outcomes classified by activating the reply-publication saga: terminal 4xx → publish_failed WITHOUT retry burn; retryable (5xx/429/network) → BullMQ attempts; ambiguous (timeout after request may have landed) → rethrow until final attempt → publish_failed (honest unknown) + reconciliation hint; GBP upsert-safety encoded (retries never duplicate). New reconcileReplyPublication use case: publish_failed guard → provider re-read via the sync port → provider shows reply → atomic markPublished (heals divergence); absent → still_failed; never calls replyToReview. Publish enqueue idempotent (buildIdempotencyKey → BullMQ jobId). atomic-review-outbox guard extended: emitAndRecord forbidden across ALL review-context source files. Catalogue: review.reply.* + review.expired rows now truthfully recordedInOutbox; review.updated orphan re-pointed 3.3→3.4 (inbox projection). Not claimed: durable publication state machine + scheduled reconciliation (3.8), disconnect review-purge command (3.5), tombstone intermediate state (3.8 schema evolution), reconcile operator surface (3.8/BQC-7), other contexts' emitAndRecord removal (3.5). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Integrate delayed/system execution policy
entries.notes: INTEGRATES the BQC-2.5 contract at all three runtime choke points. NEW src/shared/jobs/delayed-execution-gate.ts is the single decision point: catalogue-derived name→action/scope maps; GateOutcome mapping (allow → invoke; policy_unavailable → deny_retry → call site throws so BullMQ retries — unavailable policy is transient, never silent success; every other deny → deny_terminal, no side effect). (1) Worker dispatch: both BullMQ workers use createGatedJobHandler — covers 19 jobs + 12 schedules (repeatJobKey/'-recurring' classification → executionKind schedule, principal schedule:<name>). (2) Event bus: on() carries catalogue consumer identity (51 registrations across 8 tables), emit() authorizes per handler — the 3 dark bus consumers (goal/badge/leaderboard) now deny at emit time; their catalogue capabilities corrected 'none'→goal.use/badge.use/leaderboard.use so the policy can deny. (3) Durable dispatcher: gate after receipt check before handler; terminal deny skips without receipt (denied-receipt status = BQC-3.6). All 40 delayed catalogue rows flipped pending_bqc3→integrated_bqc3 (guard-enforced). Typed outcomes proven by 40 new tests: revocation-while-queued (real PG: allow → suspend → deny_terminal/org_suspended), unavailable-policy (deny_retry), manual-enqueue initiator (audit row: principal + correlation, content-free), dark-runtime denial (bus + jobs), stale_context annotation, plus the 2.5 DELAYED_CONTRACT_FIXTURES driven through gateJob. Envelope: policy?: JobPolicyContext on sync/publish/import/urgent-email payloads; initiator stamped at approveReply/retryPublish (user), startPropertyImport (user), GBP webhook (system webhook:gbp). Tenant-cross work decides under the documented TENANT_CROSS_ORG sentinel (2.5 fixture pattern). publish-reply scope resolved at dispatch (reply→review→propertyId); import-property catalogue row re-scoped property→organization (org fan-out; per-property authz lives in the sync jobs). BQC-0.4 in-handler capability checks REMOVED from the 3 GBP job handlers — new architecture test delayed-policy-delegation bans direct policy imports in *.job.ts: the gate is the single decision point. Deviations/deferred: policyVersionAtEnqueue not stamped (store snapshot number ≠ EXECUTION_POLICY_VERSION — stamping would falsely annotate stale; needs a contract accessor, deferred); initiator not persisted in audit (no column — would extend the 2.5 contract + migration); dispatcher consumer module hardcoded 'inbox.outbox-consumers' (registration carries no module field — 3.4/3.5 must thread it when adding consumers); process-image property-scope trap recorded (blocked capability denies first; portal promotion owns); activity/notification insert payloads unstamped (already carry eventId); unknown-job quarantine + terminal-deny receipt status = 3.6. Not claimed: durable dispatcher enablement (3.9), family migrations (3.3–3.5), dispatcher correction (3.6). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Inventory and register enabled event/job families
entries.notes: 54 event families + 19 job families catalogued in src/shared/governance/event-job-catalogue.ts, guard-verified bidirectionally against code (event-job-catalogue.test.ts, 17 tests): _tag discovery ⇔ rows, schemaRegistered flags ⇔ schema-registrations.ts scan, producer files must contain the event literal, consumer sets mechanically derived from .on()/eventType scans (bus vs durable), jobs ⇔ bootstrap registry with constant resolution, schedules ⇔ worker/index.ts, cross-checked against the 2.1 entry-point catalogue job/consumer/schedule rows. Dispositions: 26 enabled events (each with ≥1 consumer — the readiness rule is guard-enforced), 7 orphans each pinned to an owning slice (review.updated → 3.3; identity.organization.created, identity.invitation.rejected, property.updated/deleted, integration.property_import.completed, integration.google_connection.visibility_changed → 3.5), 21 denied_dark (team/portal/guest/goal/badge). Jobs: 12 enabled, 4 denied_dark, 3 blocked_capability (portal.upload + notification.send_email ×2; blocked wins over dark when both apply). Dark consumer refs pinned per module as the 3.2 runtime-denial checklist (badge/goal/leaderboard handlers on metric.recorded). Region recorded 'unscoped' pending BQC-4; retry policy resolved per job (publish-reply the only non-default backoff). Surfaced for later slices (recorded, not fixed): guest emitted names never match registered schemas (guest.scanned/rated/feedback_submitted are dead registrations; guest.review_link.clicked unregistered); schema/event field mismatches that will throw at outbox insertion on the emitAndRecord path (staff.assigned/unassigned staffId, inbox assign/escalation/note/bulk fields, metric.recorded recordedAt, portal.deleted propertyId — static analysis, runtime confirmation belongs to 3.3–3.5); goal.completed + integration.property_import.completed + adapter-emitted property.created are bus-only (never recorded); inbox durable consumers commit state and receipt in separate awaits (3.4 atomicity); projection-contract.ts sourceEvents names stale. Not claimed: runtime denial of dark handlers (3.2), atomic command/projection fixes (3.3–3.5), dispatcher/unknown-work correction (3.6). Slice not accepted — pending independent review.

entries.title: Controlled pilot and AI-readiness handoff
entries.blockedDependency: BQC-0 through BQC-8 accepted + human pilot authorization
